channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: False nines or firing forwards - England's potential life after Kane
item.description: Are England facing the prospect of using a false nine up front once record scorer Harry Kane calls time on his international career?

item.title: The Bronze Final - the unwanted tie or a match with a 'golden layer'?
item.description: Why does the Fifa World Cup Bronze Final still exist?

item.title: Will the World Cup affect Arsenal's transfer plans?
item.description: With the World Cup final taking place on Sunday and the start of the Premier League just over a month away, supporters are starting to think about how their side is shaping up for the new campaign.

item.title: The harrowing dark side of England's World Cup exit
item.description: During a major football tournament such as the World Cup, some women and girls are constantly living in fear - because domestic abuse rises when England play, research shows.

item.title: Messi and Brady in disbelief over 'prophetic' Lamine Yamal photo
item.description: Argentina's Lionel Messi says it is "crazy" to be facing Spain's Lamine Yamal in a World Cup final after being photographed with him as a baby.

item.title: Inside Iraola's first week at Liverpool and big decisions he faces
item.description: New Liverpool manager Andoni Iraola has lots of decisions to make ahead of the new season. BBC Sport gives an insight into his first week in the job.

item.title: Huge rise in harmful social media posts this World Cup
item.description: There have been 14 times more harmful social media posts and comments during this World Cup than in 2022, Fifa finds.

item.title: World Cup winner Capdevila appeals to Trump after US travel permit denied
item.description: Former Spain international Joan Capdevila has made a plea to US President Donald Trump for help after he says he was denied a travel permit to enter the United States before Sunday's World Cup final.

item.title: The classroom moment that bonds finalists De la Fuente & Scaloni forever
item.description: Spain manager Luis de la Fuente and Argentina boss Lionel Scaloni will go head-to-head in the World Cup final on Sunday - and the two men know each other well.

item.title: All 11 last-minute winners at the World Cup
item.description: Watch all 11 winning goals scored in stoppage time at the 2026 Fifa World Cup.

item.title: Argentina shatter England's World Cup dreams in semi-final
item.description: Argentina shatter England's World Cup dreams, scoring two late goals to come from behind and knock out Thomas Tuchel's side, in a dramatic semi-final in Atlanta.

item.title: 'No regrets' - Tuchel on England's defeat by Argentina
item.description: England manager Thomas Tuchel believes his team "got too passive" after scoring first in the World Cup semi-final against Argentina, which they lost 2-1 at Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: 'Gutted' - Kane disappointed over World Cup exit
item.description: England captain Harry Kane gives his analysis on their defeat against Argentina in the 2026 World Cup semi-final at Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: Late Martinez header sends Argentina to World Cup final
item.description: Substitute Lautaro Martinez is in the right place at the right time to head home Lionel Messi's 92nd-minute cross to send Argentina to the World Cup final at the expense of England.

item.title: Argentina celebrate progressing to World Cup final
item.description: Defending champions Argentina celebrate at full time, after beating England 2-1 to make it through to their second successive World Cup final.

item.title: A bit 'blah' or a worthy host? Is MetLife right World Cup final choice?
item.description: North America correspondent Nada Tawfik explores the decision to award the World Cup final to MetLife Stadium amid competition from rival venues.

item.title: Quiz: Name every men's World Cup final host city
item.description: The World Cup final is where heroes are made and dreams come true for players and fans alike. Can you name every city that has ever hosted the showpiece?

item.title: How Villa's record signing Manzambi can spearhead new era
item.description: It is a new era for Aston Villa - and Johan Manzambi, who has just joined in a club record deal worth more than £50m - will be one of the poster boys.

item.title: Defiant Tuchel defends decisions and says loss is 'scar we carry now'
item.description: England head coach Thomas Tuchel defends his tactical decisions during the World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina in a tense news conference.

item.title: No panic, but Celtic not good enough yet - Lambert
item.description: Paul Lambert believes former club Celtic are "nowhere near good enough" without further recruitment but has guarded against "panic".

item.title: Argentina like to leave mark on opponents - Laporte
item.description: Spain defender Aymeric Laporte claims World Cup final opponents Argentina are a team that "likes to leave a mark on their opponents".

item.title: Rooney, Hart and Richards row down Hudson River
item.description: Wayne Rooney rows down the Hudson River with fellow BBC pundits Joe Hart and Micah Richards.

item.title: More games, more controversy - good and bad of biggest World Cup yet
item.description: From high ticket prices to hydration breaks, new laws and political interventions, there has rarely been a dull moment at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: 'They made him a defensive player' - Trump questions Kane role in semi
item.description: US President Donald Trump joins criticism of England's tactics in their World Cup semi-final loss to Argentina, by questioning Harry Kane's second-half "defensive" role.

item.title: Best finishers, defensive kings & runners - World Cup finalists in numbers
item.description: European champions Spain will face World Cup holders and South American champions Argentina in the final. How do they compare? BBC Sport takes a look at the numbers.

item.title: Arsenal want Villa's Rogers - Saturday's gossip
item.description: Arsenal want Morgan Rogers, Mohamed Salah is set to join Besiktas, Manchester United are interested in Neco Williams, plus more.

item.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 41
item.description: Will you get today's player in as few attempts as possible?

item.title: 'Similar to Courtois' - could Penders become Chelsea number one?
item.description: Chelsea's newest goalkeeping prospect, Mike Penders, is from the same production line that produced the legendary Thibaut Courtois.

item.title: 'Chaos v calm' - Sutton's World Cup final and third-place predictions
item.description: BBC Sport's football expert Chris Sutton makes his prediction for Spain against Argentina in the World Cup final, and the bronze final between France and England.

item.title: Fernandez fires in equaliser for Argentina
item.description: Enzo Fernandez equalises for Argentina against England in the 2026 World Cup semi final at the Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: Gordon puts England ahead against Argentina
item.description: Anthony Gordon puts England 1-0 up against Argentina in the 2026 World Cup semi-final at the Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: England and Argentina national anthems drowned out
item.description: Both England and Argentina's national anthems are drowned out by crowd noise, before the teams kick off their 2026 World Cup semi-final at the Atlanta Stadium.

item.title: Spain into World Cup final with victory over France
item.description: Spain are into the 2026 World Cup final after goals from Mikel Oyarzabal and Pedro Porro see them convincingly beat France 2-0 in their semi-final in Dallas.

item.title: How Maradona dominated England in 1986
item.description: Diego Maradona leads his Argentina team to victory against England in the quarter-final of the World Cup in 1986.

item.title: Argentina v England 2002 - Redemption for Beckham
item.description: England meet Argentina in the group stage of the 2002 World Cup.

item.title: Argentina v England 1998 - Owen, Beckham and penalty pain
item.description: England meet Argentina in the last 16 of the 1998 World Cup.

item.title: All the goals from England's 2026 World Cup campaign so far
item.description: As England gear up to face Norway at the Miami Stadium for the World Cup quarter-finals, re-live all the goals that got Thomas Tuchel's side there.

item.title: Ederson extends Atalanta contract after Man Utd move collapses
item.description: Atalanta midfielder Ederson extends his contract after a move to Manchester United collapses.

item.title: Gemmill leaves Scotland U21 role after 13 years
item.description: Scot Gemmill will depart from the role of Scotland Under-21 head coach after 13 years, the Scottish FA has announced.

item.title: Leeds sign £34.1m defender Muharemovic
item.description: Leeds United sign defender Tarik Muharemovic for £34.1m from Serie A side Sassuolo.

item.title: Five reasons for England to be cheerful despite World Cup exit
item.description: Despite the heartbreak of a World Cup semi-final loss, there are plenty of reasons to be optimistic about England's footballing future.

item.title: West Ham captain Bowen to stay at club
item.description: West Ham United captain Jarrod Bowen will stay at the club for the forthcoming season in the Championship.

item.title: Stick with Tuchel unless Guardiola is available - Rooney
item.description: Wayne Rooney thinks the Football Association should stick with Thomas Tuchel as England head coach unless a move for Pep Guardiola is possible.

item.title: Are Argentina being treated favourably at World Cup?
item.description: Egypt have alleged bias in favour of Argentina and favouritism towards their star player, Lionel Messi. Is this fair?

item.title: Keep calm and carry on - Spain trust team identity to deliver World Cup
item.description: Former Spain captain Cesar Azpilicueta explains why Luis de la Fuente's side have confidence in the collective as they prepare for Sunday's World Cup final against Argentina.

item.title: Pele at the Azteca? England in '66? Ranking the best World Cup finals
item.description: Pele and Brazil at the Azteca in 1970? Lionel Messi's first World Cup? England winning it on home soil in 1966? BBC Sport attempts to rank the best finals.

item.title: Villa complete club record Manzambi signing
item.description: Unai Emery makes his first summer signing by bringing in Johan Manzambi for a club record fee of just over £50m.

item.title: Effort, spirit and moments - but do England lack all-round quality?
item.description: As England reflect on their World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina, BBC Sport's Phil McNulty looks at what Thomas Tuchel's team were lacking.

item.title: Iheanacho rejects new Celtic deal as Saracchi gives up on return
item.description: Nigeria striker Kelechi Iheanacho turns down a new contract with Celtic and joins Bursaspor as former loanee Marcelo Saracchi admits defeat in his bid to return to the Scottish champions.

item.title: Who could follow as Gordon leaves Scotland as a legend?
item.description: Craig Gordon has retired as a goalkeeping legend with Heart of Midlothian and Scotland, but what is his legacy and who can succeed him with the national team?

item.title: How World Cup final could be pivotal in Ballon d'Or race
item.description: With the Ballon d'Or race tighter than ever before, BBC Sport looks at how the World Cup final could determine the 2026 winner.

item.title: 'A true miracle of destiny' - Messi and Lamine Yamal's incredible first meeting
item.description: BBC Sport explores a bond between two world-class footballers from different generations who appear bound by destiny

item.title: Why you should care about World Cup third-place play-off
item.description: It might feel like the last thing anyone wants right now - but here is why, actually, you do want to watch the World Cup's third-place play-off.

item.title: 60 years of hurt - England's World Cup wait in numbers
item.description: England’s wait for another World Cup triumph wait told through the football, people and moments that have shaped the world since 1966.

item.title: How coach-on-pitch Messi undid England's tactics on the fly
item.description: England pride themselves on their physicality, so shouldn't it have suited them to face a 39-year-old who spends most of his time walking? Not this 39-year-old. BBC Sport football tactics correspondent Umir Irfan explores a Lionel Messi masterclass.

item.title: Borrowed shoes, no Golden Boot - the story of the World Cup's greatest scorer
item.description: Just Fontaine's record of 13 goals in a single World Cup has stood for decades, but he never played in the tournament again after injury ended his career at the age of 28.

item.title: When the World Cup finishes, the football continues on BBC Sport
item.description: When the World Cup ends we'll still have loads of great content every day as we build up to the new football season.

item.title: All done deals in July 2026
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: What is the World Cup prize money?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team break down the prize money on offer at the 2026 World Cup.

item.title: What are the key dates for the 2026-27 football season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything explains the full list of key dates for the 2026-27 men's football season

item.title: Should teams bring on players just for penalty shootouts?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at the numbers behind late substitutes taking penalties in shootouts.

item.title: Who has been eliminated from the World Cup?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team lists the full set of nations who have been eliminated from the 2026 World Cup so far

item.title: How are the TV picks decided for the World Cup's round of 32?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains the complexities behind television selections for the 2026 World Cup's round of 32 fixtures

item.title: Is five-match ban for Qatar's Madibo excessive?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team look at whether Madibo's punishment for his challenge on Kone was proportionate.

item.title: Who is the African World Cup goalscorer older than Ronaldo and Messi?
item.description: While Cristiano Ronaldo and Lionel Messi appear to be in a personal battle to rewrite the World Cup record books, there is one landmark that appears beyond them.

item.title: 10-man Kilmarnock leave it late against Elgin - League Cup round-up
item.description: Ten-man Kilmarnock need a stoppage-time goal to defeat Elgin City in the Premier Sports Cup group stage, while St Johnstone and holders St Mirren are also among Saturday's winners.

item.title: Hassan priority target for Celtic - gossip
item.description: Winger becomes key Celtic target, Aberdeen comings and goings and Motherwell agree deal to sell winger.

item.title: Hearts sign goalkeeper Reus as Schwolow departs
item.description: Heart of Midlothian announce the imminent departure of Alexander Schwolow and the arrival of a replacement goalkeeper, Beau Reus.

item.title: Who has your Premiership club brought in & let go this summer?
item.description: Your one-stop shop for all the ins and outs in the Scottish Premiership during the 2026 summer transfer window.

item.title: Aston Villa sign defender Harviken from Juventus
item.description: Aston Villa have signed Norway international Mathilde Harviken from Juventus.

item.title: West Ham sign forward Gago and defender Cascarino
item.description: West Ham sign Everton striker Kelly Gago and turn fellow France international Estelle Cascarino's loan move into a permanent deal.

item.title: Arsenal sign teenage Danish goalkeeper Damm
item.description: Arsenal have signed Danish youth international goalkeeper Isabella Damm on a permanent transfer from Brondby IF.

item.title: Birmingham City sign Canada defender Levasseur
item.description: WSL newcomers Birmingham City have signed Canada international Marie Levasseur.

item.title: Record crowd as Kerr returns to Gotham FC
item.description: Former Chelsea forward Sam Kerr makes her second debut for Gotham FC in front of the largest ever crowd for a women's sporting event in New York City.

item.title: French defender Rastocle signs for Birmingham
item.description: Birmingham City sign French defender Jade Rastocle from Montpellier for an undisclosed fee.

item.title: Palace sign England Under-23 striker Lloyd-Smith
item.description: Crystal Palace sign last season's WSL2 top scorer Lexi Lloyd-Smith on a three-year deal.

item.title: In spotlight but shunning limelight - Olise's unique rise to top
item.description: A footballer who dislikes too much media attention, but Michael Olise might not have much choice if he carries on his World Cup heroics.

item.title: How Cornish miners brought football to Mexico
item.description: As Mexico prepares to become the first country to host or co-host the World Cup three times, BBC Sport looks at its Cornish footballing roots.

item.title: The summer a golden generation lost its shine
item.description: The summer of 2006 was meant to be England's moment. A world-class squad, celebrity partners and confident fans - the hype was huge and the fall was hard.

item.title: How Toone is navigating grief through football
item.description: England and Manchester United midfielder Ella Toone opens up about grief, football and preparing for her wedding without her dad in a new BBC documentary.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.
